generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder for motor feedback with 0.06 mm repeatability, used for accurate joint position tracking in closed-loop control systems. Part of the dual-encoder arrangement to reduce backlash."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary high-precision position encoder providing redundant position measurement for motor axis, working in tandem with the primary encoder to minimize backlash and improve positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current measurement sensor capturing motor current for torque estimation and friction modeling in the drive axis system. Provides feedback for adaptive control mechanisms."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance CurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PositionalAccuracyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model simulating joint position dynamics with 0.06 mm repeatability constraints, incorporating dual-encoder feedback for backlash compensation and closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PositionalAccuracyEnabler
]

instance TorqueEstimationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven model using current sensor measurements to estimate motor torque and model friction characteristics for adaptive control adjustments in the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueEstimationEnabler
]

instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated positional data traces from dual encoders, processed for noise reduction and abstraction into meaningful representations for control system validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted, SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PositionalAccuracyEnabler
]

instance CurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed current measurements used for torque estimation and friction modeling, aggregated to support adaptive control strategies in the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueEstimationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PositionalAccuracyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based processing component implementing the RFLP methodology for hierarchical modeling of positional accuracy requirements, functions, and physics-based simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables PositionalAccuracyService
]

instance TorqueEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink-based simulation engine processing current data traces through machine learning models for friction compensation and torque estimation."
    DTDFVocab:enables TorqueEstimationService
]

instance MQTTDataIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data ingestion framework streaming sensor data from physical system to digital twin computational models with real-time or near-real-time processing capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService
]

// C6: Services
instance PositionalAccuracyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time positional accuracy monitoring service providing fine-grained traceability of joint positions through dual encoder feedback and physics-based simulations."
    DTDFVocab:provides PositionalAccuracyInsight
]

instance TorqueEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Torque estimation and friction modeling service using current sensor data to optimize control parameters and predict system behavior under varying operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueEstimationInsight
]

instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Streaming data ingestion service handling MQTT-based sensor data transmission from physical system to digital twin processing components with event-driven processing capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataIngestionInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance PositionalAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing 0.06 mm repeatability validation and backlash compensation analysis through dual encoder data aggregation and physics-based modeling."
]

instance TorqueEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight delivering torque estimation results and friction modeling outputs for adaptive control parameter adjustments in the digital twin system."
]

instance DataIngestionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight confirming successful data transmission from physical system to digital twin with real-time processing capabilities for contextual data trace aggregation."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance PMSMMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) integrated with cycloidal gear transmission system for high-precision actuation in the MABI MAX industrial robot."
    base:isContainedIn IndustrialRobotEnvironment
]

instance CycloidalGearSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-reduction cycloidal gear transmission system providing sub-millimeter positional accuracy (= 0.1 mm) through precise gearing mechanisms and encoder feedback."
    base:isContainedIn IndustrialRobotEnvironment
]

// C12: Control Components
instance SiemensController : DTDFVocab:ControlComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller managing joint range constraints [-90°, 90°] and enforcing safety limits through closed-loop control mechanisms."
    base:isContainedIn IndustrialRobotEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlEnabler
]

// C11: Additional Enablers
instance ControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Field-oriented control (FOC) implementation framework integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl for precise motor control and safety cell boundary enforcement."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling framework implementing internal block diagrams (IBDs) and architecture description languages (ADLs) for component-connector modeling of the digital twin system."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureService
]

// C6: Additional Services
instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active closed-loop control service adjusting actuator parameters in real-time based on digital twin insights and physical system feedback for sub-millimeter positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAction
]

instance ArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "System architecture modeling service providing functional/logical layer representations through SysML IBDs and ADLs for hierarchical digital twin modeling."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureInsight
]

// C17: Additional Actions
instance ControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of actuator parameters based on digital twin torque estimation and positional accuracy insights to maintain sub-millimeter precision."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing hierarchical system architecture representations through SysML-based modeling of requirements, functions, logic, and physics layers."
]

// C18: Environment
instance IndustrialRobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the MABI MAX industrial robot with PMSM actuators, cycloidal gear systems, and safety cell constraints [-90°, 90°]."
    base:contains PMSMMotorActuator, CycloidalGearSystem, MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder, CurrentSensor, SiemensController
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Virtual environment containing processing components, models, and services implementing the digital twin system for real-time monitoring, optimization, and control."
    base:contains PositionalAccuracyEnabler, TorqueEstimationEnabler, MQTTDataIngestionEnabler, PositionalAccuracyModel, TorqueEstimationModel, PositionalAccuracyService, TorqueEstimationService, DataIngestionService, ControlEnabler, SysMLModelingEnabler, ControlService, ArchitectureService
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's temporal aspects are governed by the **one-way data flow** between the physical system and its digital representation, as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model [32]. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed, the model implies **real-time or near-real-time traceability** for data traces, where input data traces (e.g., sensor readings, operational logs) are aggregated and processed by the **Processing component** [33]. This component consumes input data traces and produces output data with **fine-grained traceability**, ensuring that temporal dependencies between physical events and digital abstractions are preserved. The reference model does not specify granularity thresholds but emphasizes **transitive traceability**—meaning computed data traces retain their temporal lineage from source inputs. For engineering models (e.g., structure, behavior), the system may integrate with **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink [28]) or **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) to enforce time-scale constraints during computational processing. However, no explicit time-scale metrics (e.g., Hz, ms) or latency SLAs are mentioned in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration is framed within the **Digital Shadow Reference Model’s concretization**, which adheres to a **reference model-conforming architecture** [41]. The system’s **orchestration** relies on:
   1. **Component Integration**: The **Processing component** acts as the core orchestrator, consuming input data traces (e.g., sensor data, operational logs) and producing output traces via **data calculation or simulation models** [33]. This component integrates with **heterogeneous tools** (e.g., SysML [17], Simulink [28], C++/Java) to enforce behavioral and structural consistency.
   2. **Resource Management**: The model does not specify explicit resource allocation strategies but implies **traceability-driven resource usage**, where data traces are linked to their **originating processing components** and models [16]. This ensures accountability for computational resources.
   3. **System-Wide Coordination**: While no explicit patterns (e.g., event-driven, service-oriented) are mentioned, the **purpose-driven design** [42] suggests coordination is aligned with **use-case-specific requirements**, leveraging **association-based traceability** to maintain consistency across distributed elements. The **conforming model** (e.g., UML Class Diagrams) encapsulates relationships between components, ensuring end-to-end orchestration. No mention of **architecture patterns** (e.g., event bus, peer-to-peer) or **standardized protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as implied by the provided documents, is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. The lifecycle also incorporates **concretization of reference models** (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model) into domain-specific conforming models, where **Class Diagrams (CDs)** map reference concepts to concrete elements via **conformance relations** (e.g., name alignment or explicit mappings). Governance is implied through **traceability mechanisms** (e.g., transitive traceability of data traces to processing components and models) and **purpose-driven design** (e.g., aligning DS/DT creation with specific use-case requirements)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin’s **fidelity and validity** depend on the **correctness of integrated models**, **data traceability**, and **uncertainty quantification** in both analytical and data-driven components. Key considerations include:
- **Model fidelity requirements**:
  - **Physics-based models** (e.g., Simulink simulations) must replicate **nonlinear friction phenomena** with high accuracy, leveraging **white-box approaches** for interpretability.
  - **Data-driven models** (e.g., machine learning) are employed to **refine predictions** for complex behaviors (e.g., stick-slip friction) where analytical models fall short.
- **Validation and verification methods**:
  - **Deviation analysis**: Continuous comparison between **simulated data** (from white-box models) and **measured data** (from live traces) to identify discrepancies. Errors are quantified via **root-mean-square deviation (RMSD)** metrics.
  - **Model transformations**: Verified mappings between **SysML architectural models** and simulation tools (e.g., Simulink) ensure consistency across abstraction layers.
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**:
  - **Sensor noise** (e.g., encoder resolution limits) and **model parameter drift** (e.g., gear backlash changes) introduce uncertainties, mitigated via **online calibration**.
  - **Friction variability** (e.g., temperature-dependent coefficients) is addressed through **adaptive control updates** using aggregated data traces.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**:
  - **Anomaly detection** is implicit in **closed-loop control systems**, where deviations from expected behavior (e.g., sudden torque spikes) trigger **corrective actions** (e.g., motor current adjustments).
  - **Probabilistic latent variable models** are **not explicitly mentioned** in the documents; instead, **deterministic corrections** (e.g., friction compensation) are applied.
- **Quality assurance processes**:
  - **Traceability logs** document the lineage of data traces from sensors to digital models, enabling **auditability** of transformations.
  - **Cross-validation** (not explicitly named) is implied via **real-time feedback loops** that reconcile model predictions with physical outputs.

**No explicit mention of 'Cross-validation' or 'Anomaly detection' as standalone methods; instead, processes are embedded in adaptive control and data-driven refinement.**
}"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The technical network infrastructure for this Digital Twin system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) implies a **passive data collection mechanism** without bidirectional communication. The system may rely on **standard IoT communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, as referenced in [52] for streaming sensor data) for transmitting data from physical assets to the digital representation. No specifics are provided regarding **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh), **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability mechanisms** (e.g., redundancy, failover), or **security protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication). The documents do not mention **OPC UA, Modbus, or Kafka**, nor do they describe **edge/cloud hybrid architectures** or **specific hardware/software stacks** (e.g., Mosquitto, ThingsBoard). Thus, the technical connection details are **not in Document**.
"
]

The mention of **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** and a **dashboard** (in [59]) suggests a focus on **digital shadow aggregation**, but no details on **deployment models** (e.g., containerization, virtualization) or **reliability/availability assurances** (e.g., uptime SLAs) are provided. instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the Digital Twin system is **implicitly supported** through the **Digital Shadow Reference Model’s extensibility** and **purpose-driven concretization** [41]. Key aspects include:
   1. **Data Exchange Protocols**: While not explicitly named, the model assumes **standardized data formats** for input/output traces to enable interoperability with external systems (e.g., enterprise systems, other Digital Twins). The **traceability framework** [16] ensures compatibility with **heterogeneous data sources**, though no specific protocols (e.g., REST, SOAP, DDS) are referenced.
   2. **Interoperability Standards**: The system integrates with **MBSE tools** (e.g., SysML [17], AADL [24]) and **simulation environments** (e.g., Simulink [28]), which adhere to **industry standards** for system modeling and data exchange. However, no mention of **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., ESB, API gateways) or **Digital Twin-specific standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, OMG’s Digital Twin Standard) is provided.
   3. **Dependencies**: The **Processing component** depends on **external data traces** from the CPS [33], implying integration with **sensor networks, IoT platforms, or legacy systems**. The **conforming model** allows for **customized data ingestion pipelines**, but no details on **data governance** (e.g., APIs, middleware) or **scalability constraints** are outlined.
   4. **Integration Patterns**: The **purpose-driven approach** [42] suggests integration is tailored to **specific use cases** (e.g., injection molding [42]), but no generic patterns (e.g., twin-to-twin federation, cloud-edge integration) are described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical mechanisms** explicitly tied to robotic motion control, as described in [45]. The **safety cell** of the robot restricts the maximum working range to [−90°, 90°], ensuring operational boundaries are enforced to prevent collisions or unsafe movements. Additionally, the system employs **two position encoders per drive axis** to minimize backlash and reduce positional uncertainty, contributing to deterministic safety behavior. While the provided documents do not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., role-based access control, TLS encryption) or **data governance frameworks** (e.g., Asset Administration Shell (AAS) compliance), the **RAMI 4.0 reference architecture** ([49], [50]) outlines a **layered security approach** for industrial automation systems, though its specific implementation is not elaborated in the given context. The **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]) supports model exchange for simulation, which may indirectly influence safety validation workflows, but no direct security measures are described. No mention of **MQTT security** (e.g., TLS) or **IoT platforms** (e.g., ThingsBoard) is present in the documents."
]
